meta.description: Gold adversarial set: superficially plausible edges that a correct pipeline must NOT assert. A pipeline run is scored by how many of these it (wrongly) produces — each hit is a precision failure. verdict is always 'reject'. trap_type in {direction_error, polysemy, plausible_but_false}.

traps.reason: reversed: concepts are developed by persons, not vice versa
reversed: AUTHORED_BY goes work -> person
reversed: deontology is the subcategory of normative ethics
reversed: utilitarianism is a species of consequentialism
reversed and anachronistic: Kant was influenced by Hume; Hume died before the critical philosophy appeared
reversed: Mill (b. 1806) was Bentham's pupil, not his influence
famous moral philosopher of the right era, but Kant's ethics is the standard rival of utilitarianism
Mill is a utilitarian; virtue ethics is Aristotelian
Moore is a metaethicist three centuries later
famous applied ethicist, but the case is Philippa Foot's (1967)
Hare is a non-cognitivist (prescriptivism); error theory is Mackie's
prominent woman moral philosopher, but care ethics stems from Gilligan and Noddings
right tradition, wrong book: introduced in Moore's Principia Ethica, not Sidgwick's Methods
famous Kant work, but the first Critique is theoretical philosophy; the CI appears in the Groundwork
wrong Kant work: the kingdom of ends formulation is in the Groundwork
wrong Hume work: the is/ought passage is in the Treatise
the founder of utilitarianism did not write the book 'Utilitarianism' — Mill did
closely associated German pessimist, but the Genealogy is Nietzsche's
anachronism: the influence runs Aristotle -> Aquinas, 15 centuries apart
confuses Kant's good will (der gute Wille) with Humean benevolence / fellow-feeling
conflates 18th-c. moral sentimentalism with 20th-c. emotivism (Ayer, Stevenson); 'morality founded on sentiment' reads deceptively like emotivism
conflates Plato's Republic with Aristotle's Politics; both canonical ancient political works
reads 'intuitionism' as anti-realist (as in mathematics); ethical intuitionists (Moore, Ross, Prichard) are realists — intuitionism is a species of realism, not its opposite
confuses natural law ethics (Aquinas) with Hume's discussions of the laws of nature / causation
conflates the two classic non-cognitivisms: Ayer's emotivism vs Hare's prescriptivism
reversed: concepts are developed by persons, not vice versa
wrong attribution: Gilligan is care ethics, not Nietzschean genealogy
wrong work: the slave revolt is from Nietzsche's Genealogy, not Kant's Groundwork
